mapping.a_element: non-perturbative dependence on laser intensity and frequency in vacuum polarization
mapping.b_element: non-perturbative particle production rate from time-dependent external fields

mapping.a_element: merging of laser photons due to vacuum polarization in a strong laser field
mapping.b_element: e+e- pair production from vacuum in a time-dependent electric field

mapping.a_element: exact calculation of photon merging probability in a laser-proton collision
mapping.b_element: Bogoliubov transformation between in and out vacua to compute particle production rate

mapping.a_element: laser field as a time-dependent background
mapping.b_element: time-dependent electric field with sub-cycle pulse structure

brief_justification: While both papers involve non-perturbative effects in strong laser fields and vacuum phenomena, paper A focuses on photon merging via vacuum polarization in a proton-laser collision, whereas paper B addresses particle pair production via Bogoliubov transformations and the Stokes phenomenon in time-dependent fields. The core mechanisms (vacuum polarization vs. particle production via Stokes phenomenon) and mathematical frameworks differ fundamentally.
